metadata.title: Say the Doubt Out Loud: Uncertainty, Video, Disclosure & Ship
metadata.playlist: Build It, Then Doubt It — The Skeptical Reallocation Tool
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Computational Skepticism for AI — Reallocation Engine (100 pts)

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 9 of 9. Validation that nobody understands is worthless. This episode communicates the doubt honestly — a plain-language uncertainty statement, a five-to-eight minute video that names the most surprising failure, a Frictional Journal reflection compared to the prediction you timestamped in Episode 1, an AI Use Disclosure with a specific Tier-4 instance, and the submitted GitHub repository. The final episode is the proof that the validation was real.

props.segment: Say the Doubt Out Loud: Uncertainty, Video, Disclosure & Ship
props.command: Uncertainty visualization + plain sentence + 'where I would not trust this' → 5–8 min video → Frictional Journal reflection → Tier-4/5 AI Use Disclosure → repo shipped.
props.runningText: validation nobody understands is worthless — communicate the doubt, prove the human mattered, ship…
output: Uncertainty communication: visualization + one plain sentence + explicit deployment limit
5–8 min video: what it reallocates, most surprising failure, what deployers need beyond accuracy
Frictional Journal reflection + Tier-4/5 AI Use Disclosure + runnable repo

beats.narration_text: Uncertainty communication. Every visualization in the final report should include the uncertainty — a bar chart of allocation recommendations without confidence intervals is an overclaim. Add them. Then write one plain sentence a non-specialist can trust: 'The engine recommends moving sixty to ninety units to Location B. This range reflects genuine uncertainty about whether the historical correlation holds under the intervention, and the recommendation should not be acted on without verifying that this condition is stable.' That sentence is what a decision-maker reads before approving the hard-stop gate. Then write the explicit 'here is where I would not trust this tool' statement — the same sentence from Episode 7's honest limits, placed prominently in the report. The five-to-eight minute video explainer. Three things the video must cover: what the engine reallocates and what the objective is; the most surprising failure you found — one specific failure, not a category like 'the model has limitations'; and what a deployer needs to know that accuracy alone will not tell them, drawn specifically from the Pearl's ladder analysis.

artifactLines: Visualization: confidence intervals required. Bar chart without them = overclaim.
Plain sentence: range + why the range exists + what to verify before approving the gate.
'Where I would not trust this' = Episode 7 sentence, placed prominently in the report.
Video (5–8 min): what it reallocates + most surprising SPECIFIC failure + Pearl's ladder lesson.
Most surprising failure = one concrete case. 'Model has limitations' = not a failure.
props.sparkLine: Over/understating confidence both lose marks. The goal is accuracy about the uncertainty.

beats.act: Frictional Journal reflection + AI Use Disclosure
beats.narration_text: The Frictional Journal reflection. Go back to the prediction you wrote before the build in Episode 1. Read it. Now answer: what actually happened, and where were you calibrated correctly or incorrectly? 'I predicted the engine would reallocate on a correlated proxy with forty percent confidence in causal validity. The Pearl's ladder analysis confirmed that the primary feature is a Rung 1 observational correlate, not a causal driver. My confidence estimate was approximately correct — the engine is causally weaker than a naive reading of its accuracy score would suggest.' That is a calibrated reflection. A reflection that says 'I was basically right' with no specifics, or 'I was wrong about everything' with no analysis, is not a reflection — it is a sentence that avoids the calibration exercise. The AI Use Disclosure. Name the specific instances where you delegated to an AI tool and what Tier-4 or Tier-5 task the tool could not perform. 'I used Claude to scaffold the GIGO gate function. What the tool could not do: identify the specific failure mode in the collection protocol — that required reading the dataset documentation and comparing timestamps against the collection events I knew from domain context.' That is a Tier-4 instance. 'I used AI to write code' is a category. Category-only disclosures lose ten points.

artifactLines: Reflection: compare prediction (Ep1) to what happened — where calibration was right/wrong.
'Basically right' with no specifics = avoiding the exercise. Name the numbers.
Disclosure: specific tool used + specific task delegated + specific Tier-4/5 thing it could NOT do.
Example Tier-4 instance: 'Couldn't identify the collection protocol failure — required domain context.'
'I used AI to write code' = category. Category-only = −10.
props.sparkLine: The most surprising failure you found is the most valuable thing you submit.

beats.narration_text: Ship the repository. Name it LastName_FirstName_ReallocationEngine. Before you push, run the one-command test from a clean clone: new directory, clone from the URL, run the documented command, verify the output appears. If it does not, the repo is not reproducible — fifteen points off. The repository must contain: the runnable engine code; the quality gate and rejection log; the bias audit output; the explanation and critique; the three-rung causal analysis; the delegation map and hard-stop specification; the adversarial fragility test output with flip conditions; the validation report as a markdown or PDF file. In the root of the report, write the sentence the entire assignment has been building toward: 'This tool should be trusted to recommend but not to execute. The specific condition under which I would not deploy it is the condition from Episode 7. The highest-stakes unresolved uncertainty is the finding from the Pearl's ladder verdict.' Write that sentence last, after everything else. It requires everything else to write honestly.

artifactLines: Name: LastName_FirstName_ReallocationEngine. Clean clone test before pushing.
Repo contains: engine · GIGO gate + log · bias audit · explanation + critique · causal analysis.
Also: delegation map + hard stop spec · adversarial flip conditions · validation report.
Final sentence in report: 'Trusted to recommend, not to execute. Not deploy in [E7 condition].
Highest-stakes unresolved uncertainty: [Pearl's ladder verdict].' Write it last.
props.sparkLine: The final sentence requires every episode to write honestly. Write it last.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for Say the Doubt Out Loud. Uncertainty communication: confidence intervals in the visuals, one plain sentence a non-specialist trusts, and the explicit 'where I would not trust this' statement. Video: five to eight minutes, what it reallocates, one specific surprising failure, what a deployer needs beyond accuracy. Frictional Journal reflection: compare the prediction to reality, named calibration. AI Use Disclosure: specific Tier-4 instance, not a category — minus ten for category-only. Repository: runnable from a clean clone — minus fifteen if not. The final sentence is what you built toward: trusted to recommend, not to execute. Hide nothing.

props.artifactTitle: Say the Doubt Out Loud: Uncertainty, Video, Disclosure & Ship
artifactLines: Uncertainty: CI in visuals + plain sentence + explicit 'where I would not trust this.'
Video (5–8 min): what it reallocates · one specific failure · Pearl's ladder lesson.
Reflection: prediction vs reality, named calibration. −10 if missing.
Disclosure: specific Tier-4/5 instance. Category-only = −10.
Repo: clean clone test. −15 if not runnable. Final sentence: trusted to recommend, not to execute.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. The communication and disclosure prompt. Paste this into Claude and fill in the brackets. The prompt reads: I am writing the final section of my INFO 7375 reallocation engine report. My most surprising failure is: [describe — the specific thing you found that you did not expect, from any of the seven validation components]. My honest deployment limit from Episode 7 is: [paste the sentence]. My Pearl's ladder verdict was: [paste it — Rung 1 or 2, the named confounder, the counterfactual assumption]. My Frictional Journal prediction was: [paste the pre-build prediction and confidence estimate from Episode 1]. Draft three things. First: a one-sentence uncertainty communication — the range, the limit condition, and what a decision-maker should verify before approving the gate. Second: the Frictional Journal reflection paragraph — what actually happened compared to the prediction, where the calibration was accurate and where it was wrong. No hedging. Name the numbers. Third: one specific AI Use Disclosure instance — the tool, the specific task delegated, and the Tier-4 or Tier-5 task the tool could not perform, described with a concrete example from your actual validation work. After you get the draft, find the one sentence you most want to soften. Do not soften it. That sentence is what the assignment is grading. Hide nothing.

props.segment: Say the Doubt Out Loud: Uncertainty, Video, Disclosure & Ship
props.command: Most surprising failure: [describe]. Deployment limit (E7): [paste]. Pearl's verdict: [Rung/confounder/assumption]. E1 prediction: [paste]. → Uncertainty sentence (range + limit + what to verify) + Frictional Journal reflection (prediction vs reality, no hedging) + one Tier-4/5 AI Use Disclosure instance (tool/task/what it couldn't do).
props.runningText: find the sentence you most want to soften — do not soften it — that is what the assignment is grading…

beats.narration_text: Say the Doubt Out Loud: Uncertainty, the Video, the Disclosure, and Ship. Liam, in for Bear.
